key_insight: A coupled thermo-mechanical FE model shows that 15 mm-diameter RFSSW of 6061-T6 forms through coordinated shear-extrusion, pressure-assisted bonding, and plug-riveting accumulation.
engineering_relevance: The results help predict weld quality and material flow in large-diameter RFSSW, supporting stronger lightweight aluminum joint design for load-bearing structures.
method: Built a coupled thermo-mechanical finite element model in Deform V11.0
Tracked temperature history and particle/material flow during pin-sleeve motion
Linked interface evolution and microstructure stages to the weld-formation mechanism
evidence_signal: Model predictions matched experiments closely, with a maximum peak-temperature deviation of 6 °C and a relative error below 2%.
watch_item: The mechanism is inferred from a specific alloy, tool size, and process window, so transferability to other geometries or materials should be validated.
paper_title: Thermo-mechanical numerical simulation and spot-weld formation mechanism of 15 mm-diameter refill friction stir spot welding of 6061-T6 aluminum alloy
